summary: Rootline Defense now has its first flying enemy. Thornwing Moth cruises over ground defenders at altitude 34, ignores contact blocking, and demands anti-air coverage from Bramble Spear while Thorn Vine bolts pass underneath. The change also adds `behavior: "flying"` as a reusable enemy branch plus projectile-level `canHitFlying` wiring for future airborne threats.

candidates.title: Thornwing Moth — First Flying Enemy and `behavior: "flying"` Branch
candidates.summary: Adds Thornwing Moth as the first flying enemy, introduces a reusable `behavior: "flying"` branch, ships projectile-level anti-air gating via `canHitFlying`, extends Board Scout and observation exports for flying/anti-air data, and publishes the April 18 Wings Over the Garden tutorial/challenge where Thornwings only occupy lanes 1 and 3.

keep: Flying changes what the player must cover, not just how much damage they output.
The under-flight read is visible because the moth stacks altitude offset, shadow, and bob instead of only moving the hitbox.
Board Scout explains the answer before the run starts: Flying on the enemy card, Anti-air on the plant card.
mustChange: Playwright must prove Thorn Vine bolts continue past the moth and still damage a grounded target behind it.
risks: If the altitude cue is too subtle, the miss will read as a collision bug rather than intended counterplay.

keep: A second behavior branch after sniper means enemy logic is now archetype-driven instead of monolithic walker code.
Projectile-level `canHitFlying` is the right extensibility point: future anti-air plants inherit the same surface.
Constraining Thornwing to lanes 1 and 3 keeps the board hard but teachable instead of random.
mustChange: Endless must exclude Thornwing so the memorization contract stays attached to the scripted challenge.
risks: Moving Thornwing into endless too early would turn a taught lesson into a surprise failure mode.

keep: The first flying enemy is immediately screenshot-able because the shadow and offset make the new class legible in a still frame.
Observation exports stay replay-friendly: `behavior`, `flying`, `altitude`, and top-level `projectiles[].canHitFlying` are all deterministic.
The titled board, Wings Over the Garden, links the mechanic to a daily scenario instead of a one-off sandbox test.
mustChange: Tests must lock the two-shot Bramble lethality against `maxHealth: 32` so future tuning drift is visible.
risks: If Bramble loses the two-shot breakpoint, the anti-air answer stops feeling clean.

keep: Ship a real airborne threat with a reusable `behavior: "flying"` branch and manifest-backed art.
Route anti-air through projectile data instead of plant-specific collision code.
Teach the mechanic in a dedicated tutorial and keep the endless pool grounded.
mustChange: Observation exports must include `behavior`, `flying`, `altitude`, and top-level `projectiles[]` so the new class is testable.
Board Scout must expose both sides of the counterplay: `Flying` on Thornwing and `Anti-air` on attackers.
The challenge must keep every Thornwing event in lane 1 or lane 3.
risks: Altitude legibility can fail if the shadow/offset stack is not obvious enough in motion and stills.
The no-contact flying branch must not regress existing walker/sniper behavior dispatch.
feedbackReferences: Players asked for enemies with more distinct behaviors than more walkers.
Board Scout feedback pushed the implementation to make the new counterplay visible before a run starts.

candidates.title: Wave Forecast Strip for Board Scout
candidates.summary: Adds a stronger pre-run wave timeline to Board Scout so players can see the next cluster of pressure without adding a new enemy class or a new damage channel.
keep: Improves planning without touching runtime combat.
mustChange: Needs a new strategic verb, not only better forecasting.
risks: Legible but less surprising than the first airborne enemy.

candidates.title: Second Control Plant — Root Snare
candidates.summary: Adds another control plant after Frost Fern, but extends the roster before the game has a second enemy class to justify a new answer.
keep: Builds on the reusable status-effect work from April 17.
mustChange: Needs a new enemy pressure shape to justify the added roster complexity.
risks: Another plant before another enemy class deepens the answer set faster than the question set.

winner.title: Thornwing Moth — First Flying Enemy and `behavior: "flying"` Branch
winner.summary: Adds Thornwing Moth as the first flying enemy, introduces a reusable `behavior: "flying"` branch, ships projectile-level anti-air gating via `canHitFlying`, extends Board Scout and observation exports for flying/anti-air data, and publishes the April 18 Wings Over the Garden tutorial/challenge where Thornwings only occupy lanes 1 and 3.

rationale: Thornwing Moth won because it creates the first enemy that demands a different damage channel rather than simply more damage. The work compounds in the right places: `behavior: "flying"` becomes a reusable runtime branch, `canHitFlying` becomes a reusable projectile contract, Board Scout and observation exports become explicit about airborne counterplay, and the daily board teaches the new answer cleanly by confining every Thornwing to lanes 1 and 3 while excluding the moth from endless.

feedbackInfluence.summary: Players asked for more enemy behaviors than additional walkers; flying creates a new damage-eligibility problem instead of a pure stat variation.
Board Scout feedback pushed the implementation to publish Flying and Anti-air status directly in the pre-run UI.

keyDecisions: Model Thornwing Moth with `behavior: "flying"`, `flying: true`, `altitude: 34`, `maxHealth: 32`, `speed: 52`, `breachDamage: 1`, and `score: 26`.
Branch enemy updates into `updateFlyingEnemy(enemy, deltaMs)` after the sniper branch and keep flying enemies out of blocker/contact logic.
Render altitude with sprite Y offset plus a Phaser Graphics ground shadow and a bob driven by scene `elapsedMs`, not by chill-scaled local animation time.
Copy `canHitFlying` from plant definitions onto runtime projectiles and skip flying targets when the flag is false.
Expose `behavior`, `flying`, `altitude`, and top-level `projectiles[].canHitFlying` in observation data for Playwright and replay consumers.
Ship April 18 as `Wings Over the Garden` with a 2-wave tutorial, a 4-wave challenge, Thornwing confined to lanes 1 and 3, and an endless pool that excludes `thornwingMoth`.
